mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity utilization approaching maximum thresholds, raising input cost pressures and prompting equity market sell-offs.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 58 percent of late-cycle periods with rising INDPRO, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear regimes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding a 0.3 percent threshold triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, increasing discount rates and forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal exhibits negative predictive power in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer spending forecasts that generate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series in predictive accuracy, or 20-day forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of confirmed tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improved household balance sheets and construction sector momentum, transmitting positive economic growth expectations to equity markets and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no directional bias when HOUST increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing real GDP expansion during a post-shock recovery phase indicates supply-side momentum and improving capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses direction during recovery phases, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of periods where INDPRO growth exceeds GDPC1 growth.
